clean_baseline_for_gap_math.source: retained fixed-router packed-sim BF16-contract S4096 run recorded in metrics.md and single_layer_gap_attribution_ladder_20260601.json
clean_baseline_for_gap_math.read: Use this clean run for the headline 68.21 ms versus 24.9 ms gap. The profiler run below is a one-iteration tracing cross-check and carries profiling overhead.

profiler_run.raw_trace_policy: torch_trace.json and torch_key_averages.txt are retained outside the repo; this artifact keeps compact derived numbers only.
profiler_run.command_shape: python3 dsv4_layer_microbench/profile_microbench.py --preset full --impl fp4-packed-sim --moe-impl grouped-expert-batches-cyclic-uniform-dense-wgrad-baddbmm-xgrad --shared-expert-impl linear-pair --attention-impl sparse-gather-manual-bwd-split-comp-no-kv-cat-prealloc-bmm-out-comp-sel-baddbmm-triton-softmax-fwd-bwd-fast-triton-rope-qnorm-bf16-contract-window-tail-sum --batch 1 --seqlen 4096 --dtype bfloat16 --device cuda --warmup 1 --iters 1 --fixed-router --rmsnorm-save-policy saved-accum --boundary-impl separate --trace-dir profiles/s4096_fixed_torchtrace_20260601/torch_summary
profiler_run.warning: PyTorch profiler emitted ROCTracer duplicate flow-start warnings but wrote summary.json, torch_key_averages.txt, and torch_trace.json.

profiler_phase_ms.read: The profiler pass is about 4.2% slower than the clean 68.21 ms baseline, but preserves the same forward/backward shape: forward is near the whole layer budget and backward is far above it.

phase_kernel_family_ms.method: Summed torch_trace.json kernel durations whose timestamp falls inside the CPU user_annotation interval for phase.forward or phase.backward. This is a profiler cross-check, not a perfect synchronized subsystem tree.
phase_kernel_family_ms.read: The normal backward cross-check makes the priority clearer: backward is dominated by GEMM/BMM-family kernels, not by scalar bookkeeping. Copy/cast and scatter-like movement are material but second-order after the main math kernels.

torch_key_averages_highlights.read: The op table agrees with the phase-kernel view: BMM/GEMM families dominate, sparse MLA backward is still a large attention node, and copies/casts are visible but not the first rung.

gap_attribution_read.headline: The 68.21 ms to 24.9 ms closure is a two-wall problem: attention/LI backward and compact MoE training GEMM/backward.

attention_wall.main_measured_subwall: Compressed top-k route-slice backward: 11.02 ms out of the 14.39 ms local+compressed raw query-major slice.

moe_wall.main_measured_subwall: Padded/ragged grouped training GEMMs: segmented routed dgrad and wgrad are about 26 ms each, and the dynamic isolated bridge shows GEMMs dominate over sub-ms to about 1 ms dispatch/combine glue.

secondary_cleanup.read: Boundary/copy cleanup matters after the math walls move. It is not enough to close the gap on its own.

ladder: Fuse compressed top-k sparse-MLA backward, especially dKV/scatter and selected-group reuse.
Fuse LI target probabilities, KL derivative, and LI dQ/dK/dW with sparse MLA/compressor backward instead of running diagnostic side pipelines.
Replace padded/ragged MoE training contractions with compact-native grouped GEMM/backward kernels.
Then clean residual/RMSNorm/copy/bookkeeping around the now-smaller math kernels.
